# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### Q1. **[KTU University Exam – Dec 2023]**

**List any six anti-nutritional factors present in raw soybean and briefly state the method to inactivate them.**

**Model Answer (≈ 6 points):**

| # | ANF | Inactivation Method |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Trypsin inhibitors | Moist heat (100 °C, 10–15 min) |
| 2 | Lectins (hemagglutinins) | Moist heat (> 80 °C) |
| 3 | Urease | Moist heat (verified by urease activity test) |
| 4 | Lipoxygenase | Blanching (steam or hot water) |
| 5 | Phytic acid | Fermentation, soaking, phytase |
| 6 | Saponins | Soaking and boiling |

(Any six correctly listed with corresponding method = 3 marks.)

---

### Q2. **[KTU University Exam – May 2024]**

**Define the following terms with one-line significance each: (a) PDI, (b) NSI, (c) Urease Activity Index.**

**Model Answer:**

- **(a) PDI (Protein Dispersibility Index):** The percentage of total protein in soy meal that disperses in water under standardized conditions — measures the extent of **protein denaturation** due to heat.
- **(b) NSI (Nitrogen Solubility Index):** The fraction of total nitrogen in soy meal that is soluble in water — indicates **protein solubility** and native structure retention.
- **(c) Urease Activity Index:** The increase in pH (or ammonia liberated) when soybean meal is incubated with buffered urea — used as a **rapid indicator** of the adequacy of heat treatment.

---

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each)

> [!NOTE]
> **KTU ESE Rule:** Internal choice between **Question A** and **Question B**. Both are 14 marks; each contains sub-part (a) for **7 marks** and sub-part (b) for **7 marks**.

---

### **Question A (14 Marks)** — *[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024]*

**Q.A (a) [7 Marks] — Describe the complete sequence of unit operations involved in the industrial processing of soybeans for oil and meal extraction, with emphasis on flaking thickness, cooking temperature, and solvent extraction parameters.**

**Model Solution (7 marks — High-density KTU answer):**

1. **Cleaning and Drying (1 mark)** — Raw beans cleaned using screens, aspirators, magnetic separators. Dried to ≤ 12% moisture using hot-air dryers at 60–80 °C.

2. **Dehulling (1 mark)** — Beans tempered to 11–13% moisture, then passed through impact huller or disc sheller. Hulls separated by aspiration; cotyledons retained. Improves protein digestibility and reduces crude fiber.

3. **Cracking (0.5 mark)** — Corrugated roller mills reduce cotyledons to 4–6 pieces without producing excessive fines.

4. **Flaking (1.5 marks)** — Cracked pieces rolled to **0.25–0.40 mm thickness**. *Why thin flakes?*
   - Increases surface area.
   - Reduces diffusion path length for hexane.
   - Disrupts intact oil cells, releasing oil bodies.
   - Optimal thickness: balance between oil recovery and flake integrity (too thin → breakage → poor percolation).

5. **Cooking/Conditioning (1 mark)** — Vertical stack cookers (3–5 stages). Steam injection raises temperature to **75–90 °C** and moisture to **18–20%**. Functions: ruptures oil cells, denatures proteins, inactivates lipase/lipoxygenase, reduces paste viscosity.

6. **Solvent Extraction (1.5 marks)** — Hexane (boiling point 68–70 °C) percolates through flake bed in a **Bollman extractor** or **Rotocel extractor** at **55–60 °C**. Miscella (oil + hexane) collected; residual oil in meal < 1%. **Industrial recovery: ~ 99% oil yield**.

7. **Desolventizing-Toasting (0.5 mark)** — DT unit uses indirect steam at **105–110 °C** to vaporize residual hexane (to < 500 ppm) while simultaneously toasting meal for ANF inactivation. Urease activity, PDI, NSI tested for QC.

---

**Q.A (b) [7 Marks] — With the help of a flow diagram, explain the production of Soy Protein Isolate (SPI). Calculate the mass of SPI powder (containing 92% protein) obtained from 1000 kg defatted soy flour (90% dry solids, 52% protein), if extraction efficiency is 85% and isoelectric recovery is 95%.**

**Model Solution:**

**Process Steps (3 marks):**

1. **Alkaline extraction** — Defatted flour dispersed in water (1:10 to 1:15) at **pH 9–10**, **50 °C**, **30 min**. Proteins dissolve.
2. **Centrifugation** — Insoluble fiber and carbohydrates removed; supernatant contains soluble proteins.
3. **Isoelectric precipitation** — pH adjusted to **4.5** using **1 N HCl**. Proteins coagulate as curd; supernatant (whey) decanted.
4. **Recovery and neutralization** — Curd washed, re-dispersed in water, neutralized to **pH 7.0** with NaOH.
5. **Spray drying** — Atomized at **inlet 180 °C / outlet 80 °C** → fine SPI powder (~ **92% protein**).

**Numerical Part (4 marks):**

$$\begin{aligned}
m_{\text{dry flour}} &= 1000 \times 0.90 = 900 \text{ kg} \\
m_{\text{initial protein}} &= 900 \times 0.52 = 468 \text{ kg} \\
m_{\text{extracted}} &= 468 \times 0.85 = 397.8 \text{ kg} \\
m_{\text{recovered}} &= 397.8 \times 0.95 = 377.91 \text{ kg protein} \\
m_{\text{SPI}} &= \dfrac{377.91}{0.92} = \boxed{410.77 \text{ kg SPI powder}}
\end{aligned}$$

---

### **Question B (14 Marks)** — *[KTU University Exam – Dec 2024 — Alternate Choice]*

**Q.B (a) [7 Marks] — Explain the different methods of producing Soy Protein Concentrate (SPC). Compare SPC and SPI in terms of protein content, production method, and functional applications.**

**Model Solution (7 marks):**

**SPC Definition (0.5 mark):** Product containing ≥ **70% protein** (dry basis), produced by removing soluble carbohydrates and non-protein constituents from defatted soy flakes.

**Methods of Production (4 marks):**

| # | Method | Principle | Merits |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Acid leaching** | Insoluble carbohydrates at **pH 4.5 (protein pI)**; proteins retained | High NSI retention |
| 2 | **Alcohol extraction (60–80% ethanol)** | Dissolves sugars, some proteins, flavors | Mild flavor, low ANF |
| 3 | **Hot water leaching (80 °C)** | Denatures proteins → insoluble, leaches sugars | High yield, no chemicals |
| 4 | **Wet milling + membrane filtration** | Selective separation using UF/NF | Low effluent, modern |

**Comparison SPC vs SPI (2.5 marks):**

| Property | SPC | SPI |
|---|---|---|
| Protein content | 70–75% | ≥ 90% |
| Production | Leach soluble sugars | Alkaline extract + acid precipitate |
| Functional uses | Meat extenders, bakery, infant food | Dairy analogues, nutritional drinks, protein supplements |
| Cost | Moderate | High |
| Protein recovery | Lower | Higher |

---

**Q.B (b) [7 Marks] — Describe the preparation of Soy Milk and Tofu with a flow diagram. State the role of coagulants used.**

**Model Solution:**

**Soy Milk Preparation (3 marks):**

1. **Cleaning and Soaking** — Whole soybeans cleaned and soaked in water (1:3 w/v) for **8–12 h** at room temperature.
2. **Grinding** — Wet grinding with hot water (80–90 °C) at **1:8 (beans:water)** using a stone mill or stainless-steel grinder.
3. **Boiling** — Slurry boiled for 10–15 min to inactivate lipoxygenase (eliminates beany flavor) and trypsin inhibitors.
4. **Filtration** — Filtration through muslin/centrifuge removes **okara** (insoluble residue). Filtrate = **soy milk** (~ 3–4% protein).

**Tofu Preparation (3 marks):**

1. **Coagulation** — Soy milk heated to **70–80 °C**. Coagulant added slowly with gentle stirring. Curd forms within 10–20 min.
2. **Molding** — Curd ladled into muslin-lined perforated boxes, pressed to expel whey.
3. **Setting** — Pressed tofu block cooled, cut, and stored in chilled water.

**Coagulants and Their Roles (1 mark):**

| Coagulant | Role / Effect |
|---|---|
| **Calcium sulphate (CaSO₄ · 2H₂O) — nigari** | Produces firm, smooth, traditional-style tofu |
| **Magnesium chloride (MgCl₂)** | Gives soft, silky texture (Japanese silken tofu) |
| **Glucono-δ-lactone (GDL)** | Slow acidification → very soft, uniform set |
| **Citric / lactic acid** | Sharp sour flavour, used in some variants |

---

## 5.3 ⚠️ KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Pitfall Callout

> [!WARNING]
> **Where students commonly lose marks on Soya Bean Processing questions:**
>
> 1. **Confusing SPC (70%) and SPI (90%) protein contents** — Examiner deducts 1 mark per swap.
> 2. **Forgetting the role of lipoxygenase** — Often missed; mention blanching to inactivate lipoxygenase for flavour control.
> 3. **Skipping Urease Activity Test** in oil-and-meal questions — It is the **QC gold standard**; lack of mention = 0.5 mark penalty.
> 4. **Confusing flaking thickness** — Typical answer should state **0.25–0.40 mm** and explain *why* (surface area / diffusion path).
> 5. **Not stating hexane boiling point (68–70 °C)** in solvent extraction answers.
> 6. **Skipping mass balance units** in numerical problems — Always state **kg protein** and **kg SPI**.
> 7. **Spelling mistakes** — "Hexane" (not "Hexene"), "Desolventizer" (not "Desolventiser" or "De-solventizer" — both lose spelling marks).

---

## 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

> [!TIP]
> **Last-Minute Revision — Memorize These 15 Bullets:**

- ☐ **Soya bean:** 40% protein, 20% oil, ~ 30% carbohydrates; family **Fabaceae**.
- ☐ **Key ANFs:** Trypsin inhibitors, lectins, urease, lipoxygenase, phytic acid, saponins, goitrogens.
- ☐ **Trypsin inhibitors destroyed by** moist heat (**100 °C, 10–15 min**).
- ☐ **Lipoxygenase** causes **beany flavour** → inactivated by **blanching**.
- ☐ **Safe storage moisture:** **≤ 12% (w.b.)** at **≤ 20 °C**.
- ☐ **Flake thickness for extraction:** **0.25–0.40 mm** (thin flakes = better oil release).
- ☐ **Solvent:** **Hexane** at **55–60 °C** in Bollman/Rotocel extractors.
- ☐ **Desolventizer-Toaster:** **105–110 °C** — removes hexane + inactivates ANFs.
- ☐ **Urease activity test:** **ΔpH ≤ 0.20** = adequately toasted meal.
- ☐ **Soy flour:** Defatted + ground; ~ **50–55% protein**.
- ☐ **SPC:** ≥ **70% protein**; produced by acid/alcohol/hot-water leaching.
- ☐ **SPI:** ≥ **90% protein**; alkaline extraction (pH 9–10) + isoelectric precipitation (pH 4.5) + spray drying.
- ☐ **TVP:** Texturized by **high-moisture extrusion** of defatted soy flour.
- ☐ **Soy milk:** Beans soaked → ground → boiled → filtered (okara removed).
- ☐ **Tofu:** Soy milk + coagulant (CaSO₄ / MgCl₂ / GDL) → curd pressed into blocks.
- ☐ **Fermented soy foods:** Tempeh (*Rhizopus*), Miso & Soy sauce (*Aspergillus* + yeasts + lactobacilli).
